text: Jeremy congratulates you on your new clock!
are you ready for Japan? Trip

Ceva's Theorem: In △ABC, cevians AD,BE,CF concurrent iff (AF/FB)·(BD/DC)·(CE/EA)=1. Problem: In △ABC, cevians AD,BE,CF meet at P. Given AF/FB=2/3, BD/DC=5/4, find CE/EA. Solution: (2/3)·(5/4)·(CE/EA)=1 → CE/EA=6/5. Menelaus' Theorem: Points D∈BC,E∈CA,F∈AB collinear iff (AF/FB)·(BD/DC)·(CE/EA)=-1. Problem: Line ℓ cuts △ABC at D∈BC,E∈CA,F∈AB. Given AF/FB=5/3, BD/DC=7/2, find CE/EA. Solution: (5/3)·(7/2)·(CE/EA)=-1 → CE/EA=-6/35.
